- agent-harness-construction: Design and optimize AI agent action spaces, tool definitions, and observation formatting for higher completion rates.
- agent-introspection-debugging: Structured self-debugging workflow for AI agent failures using capture, diagnosis, contained recovery, and introspection reports.
- agent-sort: Build an evidence-backed ECC install plan for a specific repo by sorting skills, commands, rules, hooks, and extras into DAILY vs LIBRARY buckets using parallel repo-aware review passes. Use when ECC should be trimmed to what a project actually needs instead of loading the full bundle.
- agentic-engineering: Operate as an agentic engineer using eval-first execution, decomposition, and cost-aware model routing.
- ai-first-engineering: Engineering operating model for teams where AI agents generate a large share of implementation output.
- ai-regression-testing: Regression testing strategies for AI-assisted development. Sandbox-mode API testing without database dependencies, automated bug-check workflows, and patterns to catch AI blind spots where the same model writes and reviews code.
- android-clean-architecture: Clean Architecture patterns for Android and Kotlin Multiplatform projects — module structure, dependency rules, UseCases, Repositories, and data layer patterns.
- api-connector-builder: Build a new API connector or provider by matching the target repo's existing integration pattern exactly. Use when adding one more integration without inventing a second architecture.
- api-design: REST API design patterns including resource naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, and rate limiting for production APIs.
- article-writing: Write articles, guides, blog posts, tutorials, newsletter issues, and other long-form content in a distinctive voice derived from supplied examples or brand guidance. Use when the user wants polished written content longer than a paragraph, especially when voice consistency, structure, and credibility matter.
- automation-audit-ops: Evidence-first automation inventory and overlap audit workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants to know which jobs, hooks, connectors, MCP servers, or wrappers are live, broken, redundant, or missing before fixing anything.
- autonomous-loops: Patterns and architectures for autonomous Claude Code loops — from simple sequential pipelines to RFC-driven multi-agent DAG systems.
- b2b-visual-design: B2B 电商视觉设计
- backend-patterns: Backend architecture patterns, API design, database optimization, and server-side best practices for Node.js, Express, and Next.js API routes.
- blueprint: Turn a one-line objective into a step-by-step construction plan for multi-session, multi-agent engineering projects. Each step has a self-contained context brief so a fresh agent can execute it cold. Includes adversarial review gate, dependency graph, parallel step detection, anti-pattern catalog, and plan mutation protocol. TRIGGER when: user requests a plan, blueprint, or roadmap for a complex multi-PR task, or describes work that needs multiple sessions. DO NOT TRIGGER when: task is completable in a single PR or fewer than 3 tool calls, or user says "just do it".
- brand-voice: Build a source-derived writing style profile from real posts, essays, launch notes, docs, or site copy, then reuse that profile across content, outreach, and social workflows. Use when the user wants voice consistency without generic AI writing tropes.
- carrier-relationship-management: Codified expertise for managing carrier portfolios, negotiating freight rates, tracking carrier performance, allocating freight, and maintaining strategic carrier relationships. Informed by transportation managers with 15+ years experience. Includes scorecarding frameworks, RFP processes, market intelligence, and compliance vetting. Use when managing carriers, negotiating rates, evaluating carrier performance, or building freight strategies.
- claude-api: Anthropic Claude API patterns for Python and TypeScript. Covers Messages API, streaming, tool use, vision, extended thinking, batches, prompt caching, and Claude Agent SDK. Use when building applications with the Claude API or Anthropic SDKs.
- claude-devfleet: Orchestrate multi-agent coding tasks via Claude DevFleet — plan projects, dispatch parallel agents in isolated worktrees, monitor progress, and read structured reports.
- clickhouse-io: ClickHouse database patterns, query optimization, analytics, and data engineering best practices for high-performance analytical workloads.
- code-tour: Create CodeTour `.tour` files — persona-targeted, step-by-step walkthroughs with real file and line anchors. Use for onboarding tours, architecture walkthroughs, PR tours, RCA tours, and structured "explain how this works" requests.
- coding-standards: Baseline cross-project coding conventions for naming, readability, immutability, and code-quality review. Use detailed frontend or backend skills for framework-specific patterns.
- compose-multiplatform-patterns: Compose Multiplatform and Jetpack Compose patterns for KMP projects — state management, navigation, theming, performance, and platform-specific UI.
- configure-ecc: Interactive installer for Everything Claude Code — guides users through selecting and installing skills and rules to user-level or project-level directories, verifies paths, and optionally optimizes installed files.
- connections-optimizer: Reorganize the user's X and LinkedIn network with review-first pruning, add/follow recommendations, and channel-specific warm outreach drafted in the user's real voice. Use when the user wants to clean up following lists, grow toward current priorities, or rebalance a social graph around higher-signal relationships.
- content-engine: Create platform-native content systems for X, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, newsletters, and repurposed multi-platform campaigns. Use when the user wants social posts, threads, scripts, content calendars, or one source asset adapted cleanly across platforms.
- content-hash-cache-pattern: Cache expensive file processing results using SHA-256 content hashes — path-independent, auto-invalidating, with service layer separation.
- continuous-agent-loop: Patterns for continuous autonomous agent loops with quality gates, evals, and recovery controls.
- continuous-learning: Automatically extract reusable patterns from Claude Code sessions and save them as learned skills for future use.
- continuous-learning-v2: Instinct-based learning system that observes sessions via hooks, creates atomic instincts with confidence scoring, and evolves them into skills/commands/agents. v2.1 adds project-scoped instincts to prevent cross-project contamination.
- copywriting: B2B 文案框架
- cost-aware-llm-pipeline: Cost optimization patterns for LLM API usage — model routing by task complexity, budget tracking, retry logic, and prompt caching.
- council: Convene a four-voice council for ambiguous decisions, tradeoffs, and go/no-go calls. Use when multiple valid paths exist and you need structured disagreement before choosing.
- cpp-coding-standards: C++ coding standards based on the C++ Core Guidelines (isocpp.github.io). Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring C++ code to enforce modern, safe, and idiomatic practices.
- cpp-testing: Use only when writing/updating/fixing C++ tests, configuring GoogleTest/CTest, diagnosing failing or flaky tests, or adding coverage/sanitizers.
- crosspost: Multi-platform content distribution across X, LinkedIn, Threads, and Bluesky. Adapts content per platform using content-engine patterns. Never posts identical content cross-platform. Use when the user wants to distribute content across social platforms.
- csharp-testing: C# and .NET testing patterns with xUnit, FluentAssertions, mocking, integration tests, and test organization best practices.
- customer-billing-ops: Operate customer billing workflows such as subscriptions, refunds, churn triage, billing-portal recovery, and plan analysis using connected billing tools like Stripe. Use when the user needs to help a customer, inspect subscription state, or manage revenue-impacting billing operations.
- customer-pitch: 客户展示思维
- customs-trade-compliance: Codified expertise for customs documentation, tariff classification, duty optimization, restricted party screening, and regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions. Informed by trade compliance specialists with 15+ years experience. Includes HS classification logic, Incoterms application, FTA utilization, and penalty mitigation. Use when handling customs clearance, tariff classification, trade compliance, import/export documentation, or duty optimization.
- dart-flutter-patterns: Production-ready Dart and Flutter patterns covering null safety, immutable state, async composition, widget architecture, popular state management frameworks (BLoC, Riverpod, Provider), GoRouter navigation, Dio networking, Freezed code generation, and clean architecture.
- dashboard-builder: Build monitoring dashboards that answer real operator questions for Grafana, SigNoz, and similar platforms. Use when turning metrics into a working dashboard instead of a vanity board.
- data-scraper-agent: Build a fully automated AI-powered data collection agent for any public source — job boards, prices, news, GitHub, sports, anything. Scrapes on a schedule, enriches data with a free LLM (Gemini Flash), stores results in Notion/Sheets/Supabase, and learns from user feedback. Runs 100% free on GitHub Actions. Use when the user wants to monitor, collect, or track any public data automatically.
- database-migrations: Database migration best practices for schema changes, data migrations, rollbacks, and zero-downtime deployments across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and common ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Kysely, Django, TypeORM, golang-migrate).
- deep-research: Multi-source deep research using firecrawl and exa MCPs. Searches the web, synthesizes findings, and delivers cited reports with source attribution. Use when the user wants thorough research on any topic with evidence and citations.
- defi-amm-security: Security checklist for Solidity AMM contracts, liquidity pools, and swap flows. Covers reentrancy, CEI ordering, donation or inflation attacks, oracle manipulation, slippage, admin controls, and integer math.
- deployment-patterns: Deployment workflows, CI/CD pipeline patterns, Docker containerization, health checks, rollback strategies, and production readiness checklists for web applications.
- django-patterns: Django architecture patterns, REST API design with DRF, ORM best practices, caching, signals, middleware, and production-grade Django apps.
- django-security: Django security best practices, authentication, authorization, CSRF protection, SQL injection prevention, XSS prevention, and secure deployment configurations.
- django-tdd: Django testing strategies with pytest-django, TDD methodology, factory_boy, mocking, coverage, and testing Django REST Framework APIs.
- django-verification: Verification loop for Django projects: migrations, linting, tests with coverage, security scans, and deployment readiness checks before release or PR.
- dmux-workflows: Multi-agent orchestration using dmux (tmux pane manager for AI agents). Patterns for parallel agent workflows across Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and other harnesses. Use when running multiple agent sessions in parallel or coordinating multi-agent development workflows.
- docker-patterns: Docker and Docker Compose patterns for local development, container security, networking, volume strategies, and multi-service orchestration.
- dotnet-patterns: Idiomatic C# and .NET patterns, conventions, dependency injection, async/await, and best practices for building robust, maintainable .NET applications.
- e2e-testing: Playwright E2E testing patterns, Page Object Model, configuration, CI/CD integration, artifact management, and flaky test strategies.
- ecc-tools-cost-audit: Evidence-first ECC Tools burn and billing audit workflow. Use when investigating runaway PR creation, quota bypass, premium-model leakage, duplicate jobs, or GitHub App cost spikes in the ECC Tools repo.
- email-ops: Evidence-first mailbox triage, drafting, send verification, and sent-mail-safe follow-up workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants to organize email, draft or send through the real mail surface, or prove what landed in Sent.
- energy-procurement: Codified expertise for electricity and gas procurement, tariff optimization, demand charge management, renewable PPA evaluation, and multi-facility energy cost management. Informed by energy procurement managers with 15+ years experience at large commercial and industrial consumers. Includes market structure analysis, hedging strategies, load profiling, and sustainability reporting frameworks. Use when procuring energy, optimizing tariffs, managing demand charges, evaluating PPAs, or developing energy strategies.
- enterprise-agent-ops: Operate long-lived agent workloads with observability, security boundaries, and lifecycle management.
- eval-harness: Formal evaluation framework for Claude Code sessions implementing eval-driven development (EDD) principles
- evm-token-decimals: Prevent silent decimal mismatch bugs across EVM chains. Covers runtime decimal lookup, chain-aware caching, bridged-token precision drift, and safe normalization for bots, dashboards, and DeFi tools.
- exa-search: Neural search via Exa MCP for web, code, and company research. Use when the user needs web search, code examples, company intel, people lookup, or AI-powered deep research with Exa's neural search engine.
- fal-ai-media: Unified media generation via fal.ai MCP — image, video, and audio. Covers text-to-image (Nano Banana), text/image-to-video (Seedance, Kling, Veo 3), text-to-speech (CSM-1B), and video-to-audio (ThinkSound). Use when the user wants to generate images, videos, or audio with AI.
- finance-billing-ops: Evidence-first revenue, pricing, refunds, team-billing, and billing-model truth workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants a sales snapshot, pricing comparison, duplicate-charge diagnosis, or code-backed billing reality instead of generic payments advice.
- foundation-models-on-device: Apple FoundationModels framework for on-device LLM — text generation, guided generation with @Generable, tool calling, and snapshot streaming in iOS 26+.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications and the visual direction matters as much as the code quality.
- frontend-patterns: Frontend development patterns for React, Next.js, state management, performance optimization, and UI best practices.
- frontend-slides: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or by converting PowerPoint files. Use when the user wants to build a presentation, convert a PPT/PPTX to web, or create slides for a talk/pitch. Helps non-designers discover their aesthetic through visual exploration rather than abstract choices.
- github-ops: GitHub repository operations, automation, and management. Issue triage, PR management, CI/CD operations, release management, and security monitoring using the gh CLI. Use when the user wants to manage GitHub issues, PRs, CI status, releases, contributors, stale items, or any GitHub operational task beyond simple git commands.
- golang-patterns: Idiomatic Go patterns, best practices, and conventions for building robust, efficient, and maintainable Go applications.
- golang-testing: Go testing patterns including table-driven tests, subtests, benchmarks, fuzzing, and test coverage. Follows TDD methodology with idiomatic Go practices.
- google-workspace-ops: Operate across Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides as one workflow surface for plans, trackers, decks, and shared documents. Use when the user needs to find, summarize, edit, migrate, or clean up Google Workspace assets without dropping to raw tool calls.
- healthcare-phi-compliance: Protected Health Information (PHI) and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) compliance patterns for healthcare applications. Covers data classification, access control, audit trails, encryption, and common leak vectors.
- hipaa-compliance: HIPAA-specific entrypoint for healthcare privacy and security work. Use when a task is explicitly framed around HIPAA, PHI handling, covered entities, BAAs, breach posture, or US healthcare compliance requirements.
- hookify-rules: This skill should be used when the user asks to create a hookify rule, write a hook rule, configure hookify, add a hookify rule, or needs guidance on hookify rule syntax and patterns.
- inquiry-analysis: 询盘分析与回复
- inventory-demand-planning: Codified expertise for demand forecasting, safety stock optimization, replenishment planning, and promotional lift estimation at multi-location retailers. Informed by demand planners with 15+ years experience managing hundreds of SKUs. Includes forecasting method selection, ABC/XYZ analysis, seasonal transition management, and vendor negotiation frameworks. Use when forecasting demand, setting safety stock, planning replenishment, managing promotions, or optimizing inventory levels.
- investor-materials: Create and update pitch decks, one-pagers, investor memos, accelerator applications, financial models, and fundraising materials. Use when the user needs investor-facing documents, projections, use-of-funds tables, milestone plans, or materials that must stay internally consistent across multiple fundraising assets.
- investor-outreach: Draft cold emails, warm intro blurbs, follow-ups, update emails, and investor communications for fundraising. Use when the user wants outreach to angels, VCs, strategic investors, or accelerators and needs concise, personalized, investor-facing messaging.
- iterative-retrieval: Pattern for progressively refining context retrieval to solve the subagent context problem
- java-coding-standards: Java coding standards for Spring Boot services: naming, immutability, Optional usage, streams, exceptions, generics, and project layout.
- jira-integration: Use this skill when retrieving Jira tickets, analyzing requirements, updating ticket status, adding comments, or transitioning issues. Provides Jira API patterns via MCP or direct REST calls.
- jpa-patterns: JPA/Hibernate patterns for entity design, relationships, query optimization, transactions, auditing, indexing, pagination, and pooling in Spring Boot.
- knowledge-ops: Knowledge base management, ingestion, sync, and retrieval across multiple storage layers (local files, MCP memory, vector stores, Git repos). Use when the user wants to save, organize, sync, deduplicate, or search across their knowledge systems.
- kotlin-coroutines-flows: Kotlin Coroutines and Flow patterns for Android and KMP — structured concurrency, Flow operators, StateFlow, error handling, and testing.
- kotlin-exposed-patterns: JetBrains Exposed ORM patterns including DSL queries, DAO pattern, transactions, HikariCP connection pooling, Flyway migrations, and repository pattern.
- kotlin-ktor-patterns: Ktor server patterns including routing DSL, plugins, authentication, Koin DI, kotlinx.serialization, WebSockets, and testApplication testing.
- kotlin-patterns: Idiomatic Kotlin patterns, best practices, and conventions for building robust, efficient, and maintainable Kotlin applications with coroutines, null safety, and DSL builders.
- kotlin-testing: Kotlin testing patterns with Kotest, MockK, coroutine testing, property-based testing, and Kover coverage. Follows TDD methodology with idiomatic Kotlin practices.
- laravel-patterns: Laravel architecture patterns, routing/controllers, Eloquent ORM, service layers, queues, events, caching, and API resources for production apps.
- laravel-plugin-discovery: Discover and evaluate Laravel packages via LaraPlugins.io MCP. Use when the user wants to find plugins, check package health, or assess Laravel/PHP compatibility.
- laravel-security: Laravel security best practices for authn/authz, validation, CSRF, mass assignment, file uploads, secrets, rate limiting, and secure deployment.
- laravel-tdd: Test-driven development for Laravel with PHPUnit and Pest, factories, database testing, fakes, and coverage targets.
- laravel-verification: Verification loop for Laravel projects: env checks, linting, static analysis, tests with coverage, security scans, and deployment readiness.
- lead-intelligence: AI-native lead intelligence and outreach pipeline. Replaces Apollo, Clay, and ZoomInfo with agent-powered signal scoring, mutual ranking, warm path discovery, source-derived voice modeling, and channel-specific outreach across email, LinkedIn, and X. Use when the user wants to find, qualify, and reach high-value contacts.
- liquid-glass-design: iOS 26 Liquid Glass design system — dynamic glass material with blur, reflection, and interactive morphing for SwiftUI, UIKit, and WidgetKit.
- llm-trading-agent-security: Security patterns for autonomous trading agents with wallet or transaction authority. Covers prompt injection, spend limits, pre-send simulation, circuit breakers, MEV protection, and key handling.
- logistics: 物流查询与出货管理
- logistics-exception-management: Codified expertise for handling freight exceptions, shipment delays, damages, losses, and carrier disputes. Informed by logistics professionals with 15+ years operational experience. Includes escalation protocols, carrier-specific behaviors, claims procedures, and judgment frameworks. Use when handling shipping exceptions, freight claims, delivery issues, or carrier disputes.
- manim-video: Build reusable Manim explainers for technical concepts, graphs, system diagrams, and product walkthroughs, then hand off to the wider ECC video stack if needed. Use when the user wants a clean animated explainer rather than a generic talking-head script.
- market-research: Conduct market research, competitive analysis, investor due diligence, and industry intelligence with source attribution and decision-oriented summaries. Use when the user wants market sizing, competitor comparisons, fund research, technology scans, or research that informs business decisions.
- mcp-server-patterns: Build MCP servers with Node/TypeScript SDK — tools, resources, prompts, Zod validation, stdio vs Streamable HTTP. Use Context7 or official MCP docs for latest API.
- messages-ops: Evidence-first live messaging workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants to read texts or DMs, recover a recent one-time code, inspect a thread before replying, or prove which message source was actually checked.
- nanoclaw-repl: Operate and extend NanoClaw v2, ECC's zero-dependency session-aware REPL built on claude -p.
- nestjs-patterns: NestJS architecture patterns for modules, controllers, providers, DTO validation, guards, interceptors, config, and production-grade TypeScript backends.
- nodejs-keccak256: Prevent Ethereum hashing bugs in JavaScript and TypeScript. Node's sha3-256 is NIST SHA3, not Ethereum Keccak-256, and silently breaks selectors, signatures, storage slots, and address derivation.
- nutrient-document-processing: Process, convert, OCR, extract, redact, sign, and fill documents using the Nutrient DWS API. Works with PDFs, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, and images.

- perl-testing: Perl testing patterns using Test2::V0, Test::More, prove runner, mocking, coverage with Devel::Cover, and TDD methodology.
- plankton-code-quality: Write-time code quality enforcement using Plankton — auto-formatting, linting, and Claude-powered fixes on every file edit via hooks.

Advisory role only — never executes the task itself. TRIGGER when: user says "optimize prompt", "improve my prompt", "how to write a prompt for", "help me prompt", "rewrite this prompt", or explicitly asks to enhance prompt quality. Also triggers on Chinese equivalents: "优化prompt", "改进prompt", "怎么写prompt", "帮我优化这个指令". DO NOT TRIGGER when: user wants the task executed directly, or says "just do it" / "直接做". DO NOT TRIGGER when user says "优化代码", "优化性能", "optimize performance", "optimize this code" — those are refactoring/performance tasks, not prompt optimization.
- python-patterns: Pythonic idioms, PEP 8 standards, type hints, and best practices for building robust, efficient, and maintainable Python applications.
- python-testing: Python testing strategies using pytest, TDD methodology, fixtures, mocking, parametrization, and coverage requirements.
- quality-nonconformance: Codified expertise for quality control, non-conformance investigation, root cause analysis, corrective action, and supplier quality management in regulated manufacturing. - ralphinho-rfc-pipeline: RFC-driven multi-agent DAG execution pattern with quality gates, merge queues, and work unit orchestration.
- regex-vs-llm-structured-text: Decision framework for choosing between regex and LLM when parsing structured text — start with regex, add LLM only for low-confidence edge cases.
- remotion-video-creation: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React. 29 domain-specific rules covering 3D, animations, audio, captions, charts, transitions, and more.
- research-ops: Evidence-first current-state research workflow for ECC. Use when the user wants fresh facts, comparisons, enrichment, or a recommendation built from current public evidence and any supplied local context.
- returns-reverse-logistics: Codified expertise for returns authorization, receipt and inspection, disposition decisions, refund processing, fraud detection, and warranty claims management. Informed by returns operations managers with 15+ years experience. Includes grading frameworks, disposition economics, fraud pattern recognition, and vendor recovery processes. Use when handling product returns, reverse logistics, refund decisions, return fraud detection, or warranty claims.
- rust-patterns: Idiomatic Rust patterns, ownership, error handling, traits, concurrency, and best practices for building safe, performant applications.
- rust-testing: Rust testing patterns including unit tests, integration tests, async testing, property-based testing, mocking, and coverage. Follows TDD methodology.
- search-first: Research-before-coding workflow. Search for existing tools, libraries, and patterns before writing custom code. Invokes the researcher agent.

lastPrompt: 我同时开多个对话让你处理任务对你的输出结果和输出速度有影响吗

content.thinking: The user is asking whether opening multiple conversations simultaneously affects my output quality and speed. This is a straightforward question about how Claude Code works with concurrent sessions.

content.text:
没有影响。

每个 Claude Code 对话是独立的会话进程，彼此之间**不共享上下文、不共享算力、不互相干扰**。你同时开 5 个对话和开 1 个对话，每个对话的响应质量和速度是一样的。

唯一需要注意的是：如果多个对话同时在操作同一个文件（比如都在编辑同一个脚本），可能会出现文件冲突。但只要各对话处理的是不同的任务或不同的文件，完全没问题。

# 对话历史记录：探索 Obsidian + Claude Code 的实战场景

## 第一阶段：理解 Obsidian 在结合中的核心价值

用户最初提问：在实际工作场景中，Obsidian 和 Claude Code 结合，Obsidian 的价值如何体现？有哪些好处和日常可用的场景？

我的第一次回答重点在理念层面：强调 Obsidian 作为本地纯文本知识库对 AI 的友好性、双向链接让 AI 产出融入知识网络、从静态仓库变为可执行环境等。给出了 4 个示例场景（开发者排错、需求转原型、自动化情报整理、技术写作），但用户反馈“太不具体”。

## 第二阶段：给出可直接执行的指令和文件格式

用户要求更具体。我调整为三个可“明天就照着做”的工作流：
- 每日零散日志自动整理成项目任务（给出具体日记格式和完整 CC 指令）
- 排错后自动生成可复用笔记（格式和指令）
- 从想法到可运行原型（算法案例）

并提供了通过 Obsidian Shell Commands 插件实现一键触发的配置方式。

## 第三阶段：用户提出四个核心思路方向

用户自己设想了四个场景，询问能否实现：
1. 让 CC 不因反复读取项目文件而占用上下文（用 Ob 存储，CC 按需读取）
2. 把项目和个人碎片化经验全部放进 Ob，让 CC 以后直接变“老油条”
3. 把个人全部信息（喜好、风格、判断、性格）放进 Ob，让 AI 给出符合自己的答案
4. 顺着这个思路还能拓展哪些应用？

我肯定这四个场景全部可实现，并给出了具体落地方案：
- 通过 `CLAUDE.md` 项目文件，用 `[[链接]]` 指向 Ob 中的文档，避免占用 token
- 统一经验笔记格式，用标签、触发场景、错误现象、正确做法等字段，并可主动注入或自动检索
- 建立 `Meta/关于我.md` 个人档案，令 CC 在关键决策前读取
- 拓展了两个方向：“角色/项目加载器”一键切换身份；“决策日志”自我演进

## 第四阶段：再拓展 5 个点为“心里去的”方向

用户要求再拓展 5 个点，但觉得之前的有点偏效率工具，没有触及更深层次。我重新思考后，聚焦在个人深层需求，给出了：
5. 职业成长的“影子导师”
6. 激活“沉睡思想”的创意连接引擎
7. 决策模拟的“平行世界推演”
8. 深层沟通与人际关系“翻译器”
9. 个人原则的“一致性守护者”

用户反馈“方向太集中了，不仅需要个人层次，还需要其他方面的拓展”。

## 第五阶段：多维度场景拓展（工作、生活、学习等）

我随后拓展了 5 个完全不同方向的场景：
11. 技术团队新人“一天上手”的智能导师
12. 对外输出的一键多平台适配
13. 数字资产的跨模态检索与重组
14. 自动化学习闭环——从阅读到应用
15. 家庭/生活管理的 AI 管家

用户再次表示“不要这样拓展”，要求直接给出“实际的案例和应用方法”，像“公司报销”那种具体问题+方案，并要天马行空列举 10 个。

## 第六阶段：10 个跨生活、工作的极端具体案例

我给出了 10 个从生活到工作、颗粒度极细的场景：
1. 公司报销发票智能整理与填报
2. 个人年度税务抵扣预估与记录
3. 搞定复杂家电/数码产品的售后维权
4. 公开演讲/内部汇报的逐字稿打磨
5. 儿童/学生假期学习计划自动化设计
6. 个人投资决策的信息聚合与情绪剥离
7. 慢性病/长期健康指标追踪与医生问诊准备
8. 复杂聚会/宴请的筹备与过敏禁忌避开
9. 任何 App 的个性化快捷键速查卡生成
10. 遗嘱/数字遗产的加密交代

用户说“工作场景占多数，再给 10 个”。

## 第七阶段：聚焦工作场景 10 个案例

我补充了 10 个工作场景：
1. 需求评审前自动生成质疑清单
2. 生产事故后的免责复盘报告
3. API 接口文档与代码的自动同步校对
4. 多项目并行的精力防冲突调度
5. 技术选型的偏见剔除决策矩阵
6. 离职调岗时平滑的知识交接流水线
7. 会议纪要的决议追踪与自动催办
8. 销售商务所需的技术应答弹药库
9. 争取资源或晋升的数据化举证报告
10. 副业/小型 SaaS 的一人公司全栈运维

## 第八阶段：工厂国际站店铺运营的 10 个案例

用户提出针对工厂做国际站店铺的场景，要求再给 10 个。我给出：
1. 产品标题自动生成与竞品关键词反编译
2. RFQ 黄金时段自动抢单 + 快速报价模板匹配
3. 客户跟进节奏自动化 + 遗忘预警
4. P4P 关键词烧钱诊断与自动停损
5. 产品详情页多语言多平台适配
6. 外贸开发信千人千面自动避禁忌
7. 智能疑难询盘专家会诊
8. 店铺评价情感分析与自动分类
9. 竞品店铺架构自动拆解与监控
10. 国际站店铺运营日报/周报自动生成

用户反馈“再给 10 个，需要适配国际站店铺运营的场景，以上说的有个别一两个不太合适”。

## 第九阶段：再次聚焦国际站运营，更精准的 10 个

我进一步细化：
1. 产品分层诊断与“爆平滞”自动分类
2. 橱窗产品位末位淘汰 + 自动推荐替换
3. 详情页行动号召按钮与关联营销配置检查
4. 询盘首轮应答自动质检
5. 平台大促本地化排期与任务清单生成
6. “零效果产品”定时清理 + 新发替代自动化
7. 买家标签化自动归类 + 跟进策略匹配
8. 行业定招技术方案速配
9. 多店铺产品标题与关键词交叉去重
10. 月度经营分析数据包自动准备

## 第十阶段：要求将对话信息总结输出

用户要求“把该对话的信息让 cc 知道，你帮我总结输出成 md”。我最初总结为一份带结论的实战手册，但用户明确指出：“不是输出结论，是输出我和你聊得客观内容，我会和cc谈论具体实践的场景和方案。”

至此，我明白了用户的意图：需要一份纯粹的对话过程记录，便于 CC 读取作为后续讨论的上下文，而不是加工后的结构化指南。这是我和deepseek网页版聊天内容，你帮我分析，作为我使用你的过程中，有哪些值得借鉴

content.text:
这份对话记录很有价值，我从中提取出几个对你使用我有直接借鉴意义的点：

---

## 你与 AI 协作的模式分析

### 1. 你对"抽象"的容忍度极低，且会持续追问直到落地

整个对话里你说了至少 4 次"不够具体""不要这样""再给 10 个"。这说明你需要的是**可立即执行的方案**，不是框架或理念。

**对我意味着**：不要给你讲道理、讲框架、讲"可以这样做"的方向性建议。直接给你具体的操作步骤、具体的文件内容、具体的指令文本。一步到位。

### 2. 你擅长自己先想清楚方向，再让 AI 验证和细化

第三阶段你主动提出四个场景思路，问"能不能实现"。你没有让 AI 替你想方向，而是自己定方向，让 AI 判断可行性和落地路径。

**对我意味着**：你是主导者，我是执行者。你给方向时我不需要帮你"拓展思维"，直接验证可行性、给落地方案。

### 3. 你会在 AI 偏离轨道时快速纠正，不将就

当 AI 给的方向偏"个人成长""生活管理"时，你说"工作场景占多数，再给 10 个"；当 AI 给出的案例有"个别一两个不太合适"时，你说"再给 10 个，需要适配国际站店铺运营"。

**对我意味着**：我做偏了你直接说，不用担心打击我。你宁愿我重做也不要我在错误方向上修补。这是效率最高的协作方式。

### 4. 你区分"客观记录"和"加工后结论"，且明确要求前者

最后你说"不是输出结论，是输出客观内容"，这是个关键信号。很多时候 AI 习惯性地把原始信息加工成结构化的"成果物"，但你需要的是**原始对话记录**，因为你自己要去和 CC 讨论这些内容。

**对我意味着**：当我需要保存或总结信息时，先问清楚你要的是"原材料"还是"加工品"。不要默认帮你提炼。

### 5. 你对工作场景的颗粒度要求很细

从"国际站运营的 10 个案例"可以看到，你需要的不是"P4P 优化"这种粗粒度建议，而是"产品分层诊断 + 爆平滞自动分类""零效果产品定时清理 + 新发替代"这种具体到操作步骤的粒度。

**对我意味着**：涉及国际站运营的建议，直接从你已有的技能文件（/p4p、/inquiry 等）和方法论出发，不说泛泛的"优化关键词"，说具体操作。

### 6. 一个高效的协作模式：你用 AI 做"可能性探索"，然后挑选落地

整个对话实际上是你在用 DeepSeek 做"场景脑暴"——不断要更多案例，筛选哪些可落地。AI 负责生成候选方案，你负责判断和选择。

**对我意味着**：当你问"还有什么可能""再给几个方案"时，这就是脑暴模式。我不需要评估每个方案的优劣（你来判断），我只需要**大量生成、覆盖不同角度、避免重复**。

---

## 总结：三条可以直接影响我行为的规则

1. **默认给具体方案，不给方向性建议**。如果你需要方向，你会问"有什么思路"；否则直接给步骤、指令、文件内容。
2. **被打回时直接换方向重来，不要在原方向上修补**。你说"不够""不要这样"时，意味着整个角度需要换，不是在同一框架里再多加两个例子。
3. **存信息前先确认你要"原材料"还是"加工品"**。你的记忆文件、项目文档大多只需要原始数据，不需要我再帮你总结提炼。
我的意思是你需要查看一下我使用你的记忆，分析我的使用习惯，在我和ds讨论的场景中，有哪些比较符合我的场景可以使用ob+cc的结合，让过程或者执行质量拔高

用户是一家母婴纺织品工厂的运营者，通过阿里巴巴国际站开展跨境B2B业务。

## 业务特点
- **产品线**：婴儿连体衣、儿童套装、布尿裤、围兜、隔尿垫、吸汗巾、纱布包单、家居服、儿童纱布服装
- **工厂**：2015年成立，500万资本，6000㎡，200+工人，BSCI/ISO9001认证
- **MOQ**：500-1000件/款
- **渠道**：阿里巴巴国际站为主要销售平台
- **三角贸易**：熟悉中国→迪拜→目标国模式

## 目标市场（基于P4P地域偏好配置）
- **核心市场**：苏丹、埃及、刚果民主共和国、赞比亚、科特迪瓦、沙特阿拉伯、阿联酋
- **辅助市场**：美国、欧洲主要国家
- **注意**：发达国家L1+以小零售商为主，与工厂MOQ 500-1000不匹配

## 技术环境
- Windows 10 + VS Code + Claude Code 扩展
- DeepSeek 模型代理
- Obsidian 知识库用于对话归档

## 沟通偏好
- 中文沟通，结构化建议
- 客户沟通需中英双语
- 注重实际操作步骤和可视化内容

7	大任务（如数据抓取、分析）必须在不同对话中执行。抓取任务一个对话，分析任务另一个对话。不要把诊断、探索、抓取、分析全塞进一个对话里。

name: 上下文管理——大任务必须分对话执行
description: 抓取/分析等大任务不能在同一对话连续执行，必须分对话以控制上下文质量

---
大任务（如数据抓取、分析）必须在不同对话中执行。抓取任务一个对话，分析任务另一个对话。不要把诊断、探索、抓取、分析全塞进一个对话里。

**Why:** 单对话持续做多阶段任务会导致上下文膨胀，模型输出和执行质量下降。抓取脚本的编写/调试在一个对话完成，实际执行在另一个对话，分析在第三个对话。

**How to apply:** 
- 完成诊断和脚本编写后，告知用户脚本路径和用法
- 提醒用户在**新对话**中执行
- 不要在同一对话中既抓取又分析
- 每次新对话开始时，先读 STATE.md 和 knowledge/ 了解状态

3	description: 阿里巴巴国际站 P4P 两计划（日预算版+智投版）的完整优化方法论，含数据采集、产品分层、地域调整、标题优化等全套决策框架
4	type: reference
5	originSessionId: 00ef2a14-f073-4f39-aca6-a3cfdd5bab7d

7	# P4P 优化技能手册
8	
9	**文件位置**: `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\P4P优化专用\P4P优化技能手册.md`
10	
11	## 核心方法论摘要
12	
13	### 两计划效率基准
14	- 日预算版单商机成本 ¥23.4，智投版 ¥40.9，**日预算版效率是智投版的 1.7 倍**
15	- 日预算版日均商机 7.6，智投版 3.0
16	- 日预算版 CTR 稳定约 3.5%，智投版近期下降
17	
18	### 产品分层（核心决策规则）
19	- 商机 ≥ 8 → 核心爆品（预算倾斜）
20	- 商机 4-7 → 优质品（正常维持）
21	- 商机 1-3 → 边缘保留（观察）
22	- 商机 0 → 待淘汰
23	- CTR > 3% + 商机少 → 有潜力继续测
24	- 曝光 < 100 → 数据不足
25	
26	### 关键避坑
27	1. **重点国家溢价不支持非洲国家** — 只能用地月偏好加
28	2. **两个计划均已设 L1+** — 不要再改人群偏好
29	3. **发达国家 L1+ ≠ B2B 买家** — 发达国家L1+仍以小零售商为主，与工厂 MOQ 500-1000 不匹配
30	4. **地域偏好**是当前最主要的优化杠杆

description: 阿里巴巴国际站 P4P 两计划（日预算版+智投版）的完整优化方法论，含数据采集、产品分层、地域调整、标题优化等全套决策框架

# P4P 优化技能手册

**文件位置**: `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\P4P优化专用\P4P优化技能手册.md`

## 核心方法论摘要

### 两计划效率基准
- 日预算版单商机成本 ¥23.4，智投版 ¥40.9，**日预算版效率是智投版的 1.7 倍**
- 日预算版日均商机 7.6，智投版 3.0
- 日预算版 CTR 稳定约 3.5%，智投版近期下降

### 产品分层（核心决策规则）
- 商机 ≥ 8 → 核心爆品（预算倾斜）
- 商机 4-7 → 优质品（正常维持）
- 商机 1-3 → 边缘保留（观察）
- 商机 0 → 待淘汰
- CTR > 3% + 商机少 → 有潜力继续测
- 曝光 < 100 → 数据不足

### 关键避坑
1. **重点国家溢价不支持非洲国家** — 只能用地月偏好加
2. **两个计划均已设 L1+** — 不要再改人群偏好
3. **发达国家 L1+ ≠ B2B 买家** — 发达国家L1+仍以小零售商为主，与工厂 MOQ 500-1000 不匹配
4. **地域偏好**是当前最主要的优化杠杆

### 店铺数据基线
- 全店 517 个产品，P4P 推广 87 个，未推广 395 个（+35个查不到数据）
- 395 个未推广品中大部分有自然搜索曝光

### 标题 B2B 关键词
高转化词: Wholesale, OEM/ODM, Custom, Bulk, Factory, Manufacturer, Supplier

content.text:
记忆读完了。以下是我的交叉分析——

---

## 先画个像：你实际怎么用我

从记忆系统来看，你的使用模式非常清晰：

| 维度 | 你的实际行为 |
|------|------------|
| **任务类型** | P4P数据分析、OKKI客户抓取/分类、询盘回复、产品文案、报告生成 |
| **协作方式** | 你定方向、我做执行；我做偏了直接打回、我换方向重来 |
| **质量要求** | 涉及工厂信息的必须先读档案原文；报告必须.docx不是.md；大任务必须分对话 |
| **节奏** | 一次一个大任务，分对话执行（抓取一个对话、分析另一个对话），靠 STATE.md 承接进度 |
| **记忆使用** | 已经建了比较完整的规则记忆（MOQ/货运报价/工厂核实/回复语言等），靠记忆系统让每次对话不用重新交代 |

你本质上是一个**单人全栈运营**——既是决策者也是执行者，业务知识全在你脑子里，你用我分担执行层面的工作量。

---

## 从 DS 讨论的几十个场景中，真正匹配你的 6 个

我按**你的实际工作内容 × OB+CC 能拔高的程度**筛了一遍：

### 1. OKKI 客户跟进节奏 + 遗忘预警（匹配度最高）

**你现在怎么做**：OKKI 432个客户分了五阶段，靠 CC 一次性抓取+分类+生成报告。但客户跟进是动态的——谁 3 天没回了？谁的阶段该升级了？这些需要持续追踪。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：OB 里每个高优先级客户一篇笔记，记录最近一次沟通时间、阶段、待办。CC 每次打开时批量读取待跟进客户的笔记，自动生成"今日跟进清单"，按紧急度排序。客户回了什么新消息，你粘贴到 OB，CC 对比历史自动判断阶段是否应该升级。

**具体 OB 结构**：
```
客户跟进/
  ├── 仪表盘.md          ← 所有客户当前阶段一览
  ├── 高优先/
  │   ├── Ahmed_Sudan.md  ← 单客户完整跟进链
  │   └── ...
  └── 战报/              ← CC 每日自动生成的跟进建议
```

**核心价值**：跟进不再靠记忆，432 个客户谁该联系了不用翻 OKKI。

### 2. P4P 关键词/产品表现的持续监控 + 异常预警

**你现在怎么做**：CC 一次性做全店 517 品分析、两计划对比，输出静态报告。P4P 是动态的——关键词烧钱速度变化、产品 CTR 骤降，需要持续盯。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：每次你从阿里后台导出最新 P4P 数据粘贴到 OB，CC 对比历史快照自动标注异常（"这个品 CTR 从 3.5% 跌到 1.2%""这个词昨天烧了 ¥40 但零商机"）。不需要每次跑全量分析，CC 只抓变化的部分。

**具体 OB 结构**：
```
P4P/
  ├── 数据快照/
  │   ├── 2026-05-01_两计划.md
  │   ├── 2026-05-08_两计划.md   ← 每次粘贴新数据
  │   └── ...
  ├── 产品档案/            ← 每个推广品的独立跟踪
  └── 异常预警.md           ← CC 对比后自动更新
```

**核心价值**：从"定期全量分析"变成"盯异常"，早发现早止损。

### 3. 询盘回复质检 + 技术应答弹药库

**你现在怎么做**：收到询盘后用 CC 生成回复，但回复质量取决于你的指令详细程度。工厂技术参数、认证列表、产能数据需要每次从档案文件里查。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：OB 里维护一个"技术应答库"——过去成功转化的询盘回复、客户常问的技术问题、每个产品线的标准参数表。CC 生成回复时自动检索应答库中类似场景的回复模板，拼接最新参数，不用每次从零起草。回复发出前 CC 自动过一遍质检清单（是否带上了认证？是否避开了报价红线？）。

**具体 OB 结构**：
```
询盘/
  ├── 应答库/
  │   ├── 常见问题_认证类.md
  │   ├── 常见问题_MOQ交货期.md
  │   ├── 常见问题_面料工艺.md
  │   └── 成功案例/         ← 实际成单的询盘→回复链条
  ├── 质检清单.md
  └── 未成单复盘/
```

**核心价值**：回复质量不再依赖你每次给的指令详细程度，知识库自动补全。

### 4. 工厂/产品知识库的结构化 + 随用随查

**你现在怎么做**：工厂信息全在一个 1.7MB 的 txt 里（`小星星工厂全息背景档案_V10.txt`），CC 每次需要时读原文。但这个文件很大，不适合频繁读取。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：把工厂档案拆成细颗粒的原子化笔记——每个认证一篇、每条产线一篇、每个产品线的参数一篇。CC 按需只读相关的几篇，不每次加载整个档案。你在运营中积累的新信息（新认证下来了、新设备到了、某个客户的特殊工艺要求做成了），直接追加到对应笔记。

**具体 OB 结构**：
```
工厂知识库/
  ├── 资质认证/
  │   ├── BSCI.md
  │   └── ISO9001.md
  ├── 产线工艺/            ← 30项工艺各一篇
  ├── 产品线参数/
  ├── 商务规则/            ← MOQ/付款条件/交期
  └── 合作客户案例/        ← 脱敏后积累
```

**核心价值**：档案从"一大坨"变成"可检索的活文档"，CC 读取效率提高，信息也能持续更新。

### 5. 月度经营分析数据包自动准备

**你现在怎么做**：CC 生成分析报告，要求输出 .docx 格式。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：每个月在 OB 里维护当月关键指标（曝光、点击、商机、成单、P4P花费）。月底 CC 读取当月+前几个月的指标，自动生成环比/同比分析，标注趋势异常，输出 .docx。不需要你每次手动整理基线数据。

**具体 OB 结构**：
```
运营数据/
  ├── 月度指标/
  │   ├── 2026-04.md       ← {曝光: xxx, 点击: xxx, ...}
  │   ├── 2026-05.md
  │   └── ...
  └── 月度报告/            ← CC 生成的对比分析 .docx
```

**核心价值**：报告从"每次从头做"变成"模板化生成"，月报 5 分钟出。

### 6. 多项目并行时，用 OB 做"对话上下文快照"

**你现在怎么做**：大任务分对话执行，靠 STATE.md 承接进度。OKKI 项目一个 STATE.md，P4P 项目没有（靠你自己记得状态）。

**OB+CC 拔高后**：每个活跃项目在 OB 里维护一个轻量状态页，记录"上次做到哪了""当前阻塞是什么""下次启动指令"。你开新对话时给 CC 一句"继续 OKKI 项目"，CC 自己去读对应笔记，不用你每次手打启动指令。

**这其实是你已经在做的事**（OKKI 的 STATE.md + CLAUDE.md 的启动指令），但可以系统化到 OB 里，所有项目统一管理。

---

## 一句话总结：对你来说 OB+CC 的核心价值不是"知识管理"，是"减少每次对话的冷启动成本"

你现在最大的操作瓶颈不是分析能力，是**每次开新对话都要重新交代背景**。OB 把业务知识、项目状态、客户历史、工厂档案都结构化了之后，CC 花 30 秒自己读完，直接进入执行状态。这就是从"每次重新教一遍"变成"它本来就懂"。

需要我把上面某个场景落地成具体的 OB 目录结构和 CC 启动指令吗？
OKKI 客户跟进节奏 + 遗忘预警（匹配度最高。该方案他能做到实时更新吗，会不会占用大量内存，他是否只能记录客户的文本消息？

content.thinking:
The user is asking three specific technical questions about the OKKI customer followup scenario:

1. Can it achieve real-time updates?
2. Will it consume a lot of memory/context?
3. Is it limited to only recording text messages?

Let me think through each:

1. **Real-time updates**: No, it can't be truly real-time. OKKI CRM data needs to be scraped (they're already struggling with this via the Playwright approach). The user would need to manually paste data or run a scraping script periodically. The OB notes would be manually updated by the user or batch-updated by CC when new data is pasted in. "Real-time" isn't the right framing - it's more like "periodic batch sync."

2. **Memory/context consumption**: This is a valid concern. If the approach is "CC reads all customer notes every time," that would blow up context quickly. The solution needs to be selective - CC should only read the dashboard/summary file and maybe the top 3-5 priority customers, not all 432. Also, OB files are plain text (small), and CC reads them on demand, not loading them all into memory. The concern is context window, not system memory.

3. **Can it only record text?**: No - OB as a markdown file system can store anything text-based: timestamps, status labels, action items, links to external resources, embedded data tables, etc. But it can't store images/audio/video directly (well, it can link to them). The user might be asking whether the system is limited to just text chat messages, or can it capture richer structured data like stage changes, followup dates, purchase intent signals, etc.

Actually, I think the user's deeper question might be about the feasibility of the whole approach - is it practical or is it going to be a maintenance nightmare? Let me address that honestly.

Let me also think about what the actual constraints are:
- OKKI doesn't have an API (that's why they're scraping)
- The data needs to be manually fetched or periodically scraped
- OB files are stored locally, no real-time sync with OKKI
- The user is a single operator, not a dev team

I should be honest about the limitations and not oversell the approach. The real value isn't "real-time automation" but rather "structured persistence so CC has context without you re-explaining."

content.text:
三个问题都问到了关键限制上，我逐一拆开说。

---

## 1. 实时更新？做不到

**OKKI 没有开放 API**，你现在的数据是靠 Playwright 模拟浏览器抓的。这意味着无论什么方案，数据都不会"自动流进来"。

实际的更新节奏只能是：

| 方式 | 频率 | 你的操作量 |
|------|------|-----------|
| 跑一次抓取脚本，CC 批量更新 OB 笔记 | 每天一次 / 每几天一次 | 一句"更新 OKKI 数据" |
| 你跟某个客户聊完，把关键信息粘贴到 OB | 随时 | 30 秒复制粘贴 |
| 完全依赖 OB 里的静态快照 | 不变 | 零 |

**实事求是地说**：这不是实时系统，是"定期同步 + 手动增量"的半自动模式。真正实时的是你打开 OKKI 看消息，OB 负责的是让你和 CC 讨论客户时不用重新翻 OKKI。

---

## 2. 会不会占大量内存/上下文？

**不会**，前提是设计对。

OB 里所有文件是纯文本 Markdown，432 个客户各一篇笔记加起来可能也就 2-3MB，磁盘占用可以忽略不计。真正要控制的是 CC 的**上下文窗口**——不能让 CC 每次打开就读全部 432 篇。

做法很简单：

- CC 启动时只读一篇**仪表盘**（所有客户当前阶段的一览表 + 今日待跟进名单，大概 50-100 行）
- 你指定"看 Ahmed 这个客户"，CC 才去读 `Ahmed_Sudan.md` 单篇
- 你说"批量生成今日跟进建议"，CC 只读仪表盘，不逐篇读客户详情

**本质是你控制 CC 读多少，不是它自己全吞。**

---

## 3. 只能记录文本消息？

不是。OB 笔记可以记录任何结构化信息，不只是聊天文字。比如单客户笔记可以长这样：

```markdown
# Ahmed - Sudan

## 基本信息
- 阶段: 样品确认中
- 来源: RFQ #20260501-003
- 产品意向: 婴儿连体衣 5000件
- 预算: $2-3/件
- 决策角色: 老板本人

## 沟通时间线
| 日期 | 事件 | 备注 |
|------|------|------|
| 05-01 | 首次询盘 | 问了认证和MOQ |
| 05-02 | 我方报价 | $2.8/件 FOB，含样 |
| 05-05 | 客户回: 价格OK要样品 | 给了DHL账号 |
| 05-07 | 样品寄出 | 运单号 1234567890 |

## 关键信号
- [x] 问了BSCI认证 → 工厂优势匹配
- [x] 问了交期 → 30天可满足
- [ ] 还没问付款方式 → 报价时主动提
- [!] 5天没回了 → 05-12 前跟进

## 下一步
- 05-10 查样品签收状态
- 签收后3天跟进试单
```

这里面有文本、有结构化表格、有勾选状态、有信号标签、有日期节点——远不止"聊天记录"。

---

## 但这个方案有一个你没问到但更关键的问题

**维护成本**。OB 里的客户笔记不会自己更新。你的实际工作流是：

1. 你在 OKKI 跟客户聊天
2. 你切到 OB 更新笔记（或粘贴聊天内容让 CC 更新）
3. CC 基于笔记给你建议

步骤 2 是**额外操作**。如果每个客户每天都更新，你会被维护笔记累死。

所以这个方案真正适合的不是"432 个客户全部追踪"，而是：

- **高优先级客户（10-20 个）**：单篇笔记，精细追踪
- **中优先级客户（30-50 个）**：只在仪表盘里一行记录状态
- **低优先级客户（其余）**：不维护 OB，偶尔需要时回 OKKI 查

**一句话**：OB 管"值得重点跟的"，不是管全部的。这样维护成本才可控。

---

要不要我把这个方案落到 OKKI 项目目录里，设计具体的 OB 目录结构和 CC 读取规则？
你想想该方案有没有更好的办法

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Here is the full report.

---

## OKKI CRM Customer Analysis Project -- Complete Architecture Report

### Project Root
`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\`

**Purpose**: Customer analysis system for Xiaoxingxing Garment Factory (小星星服饰工厂), a B2B baby/children textile OEM/ODM factory in Yiyang, China. The project scrapes data from OKKI CRM (Alibaba's Xiaoman CRM), analyzes customer conversations, classifies them into a 5-tier priority system (P0-P4), and generates actionable daily follow-up reports.

---

### 1. KEY DOCUMENTATION FILES

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\CLAUDE.md`** (5855 bytes)
The master instruction file for Claude Code. Defines:
- **Account safety rules**: Sub-account (user) vs. main account (colleague) isolation. CDP-only approach to avoid triggering SSO token cascading failures.
- **Data scraping flow**: Close Chrome, run `node scripts/scrape_okki.js`, uses Profile 4 cookies for pre-authenticated session.
- **Thinking partner mode**: Claude is instructed to act as a thinking partner, not a tool -- challenges assumptions, identifies blind spots, provides evidence-based analysis, pushes back on user judgments when data contradicts them.
- **Analysis execution rules**: Read STATE.md first, then all knowledge/ files, batch constraint of <=5 customers per batch with <=500 total messages.
- **Data verification rules** (added 2026-05-08): Anti-hallucination checks -- verify statistics before reporting, never trust `latestMessage` fields for "has customer replied" judgments, spot-check 3 samples before reporting numbers.

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\STATE.md`** (3121 bytes)
The project state tracker (auto-loaded by SessionStart hook). Current state:
- **466 total conversations** (+13 from previous 453)
- **21 changed conversations**, **1788 new messages** in latest sync (2026-05-08)
- **14 customers replied** (118 messages), 159 messages sent by our side
- Latest analysis result: P0=9, P1=14, P2=26, P3=67, P4=355
- Key findings: diego vargas (19 customer msgs, only 1 reply -- critical oversight), Mona almuharraqy (846 messages, most active but unconverted), multiple PIs awaiting payment

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\DESIGN.md`** (9511 bytes)
Full architectural design document (2026-05-04). Defines the 6-stage pipeline:
- Stage 0: Data preprocessing (Node.js) -- system message filtering, emoji/single-word detection, silence day calculation
- Stage 1: Rule engine pre-classification (Node.js) -- deterministic rules for P3/P4, marks uncertain cases `needAI: true`
- Stage 2: AI classification with two-pass method -- metadata pass first, then full content pass
- Stage 3: P0+P1 deep analysis (customer background research, 5-step analysis, personalized scripts)
- Stage 4: Full lifecycle monitoring (status machine with 14 customer states)
- Stage 5: Word report generation via `docx` npm package

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\RUNBOOK.md`** (4493 bytes)
Operations manual with 4-step workflow: API batch scrape -> TM chat exploration -> batch AI analysis -> summary report. Documents verified APIs (`customerV3Read/companyList`, `customerRead/trailList`, `customerV2Read/detail`) and session planning.

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\PHASE1_执行指令.md`** (4493 bytes)
Phase 1 TM chat API reconnaissance instructions. Step-by-step for manually finding Alibaba MTOP API endpoints, payload formats, and response structures in Chrome DevTools.

---

### 2. SCRIPTS DIRECTORY (`scripts/`)

~110 script files total. Here are the currently active/key ones, organized by function:

#### Primary Data Scraping

- **`scripts/sync_okki.js`** (20,650 bytes) -- THE MAIN SCRIPT. One-click incremental sync. Automates: CDP port detection -> Chrome launch/connect -> login detection with wait -> navigate to onetalk iframe -> extract 466 conversation metadata from `window.__conversationListFullData__` -> diff against previous `tm_conversations_full.json` -> incrementally pull chat history via LWP (Lightweight Web Protocol) over WebSocket for changed conversations. Uses CDP `Runtime.evaluate` to send LWP requests through the captured WebSocket. Saves `tm_conversations_latest.json` and updates `chat_history/*.json`.

- **`scripts/scrape_okki.js`** (6,891 bytes) -- Playwright-based general scraper using Profile 4. Intercepts all OKKI API responses, captures customer lists, supports optional customer name search. Continuous mode with 30-second auto-save intervals.

- **`scripts/scrape_api_batch.js`** (6,672 bytes) -- Batch API scraper for customer detail + email trails. Connects via CDP, calls `customerRead/trailList` and `customerV2Read/detail` APIs via `page.evaluate()` XHR. Produces `data/customers/{company_id}.json` files (346 customer files). Supports `--since` and `--ids` flags.

- **`scripts/scrape_cdp.js`** (4,142 bytes) / **`scripts/scrape_cdp_full.js`** (3,191 bytes) -- CDP-based scrapers for customer list and detail data via Chrome DevTools Protocol.

- **`scripts/quick_sync.js`** (5,943 bytes) -- Lightweight v4 sync. Opens Chrome Profile 4, waits for manual login, navigates to onetalk, intercepts both HTTP responses and WebSocket frames, saves combined capture to `data/quicksync_*.json`.

- **`scripts/extract_cdp.js`** (8,294 bytes) -- CDP-based extraction via Chrome remote debugging. Reads DOM, executes JS, intercepts HTTP responses without triggering SSO.

- **`scripts/extract_playwright.js`** (7,884 bytes) -- Playwright-based extraction. Programmatically navigates customer pages, intercepts XHR/fetch, captures API responses.

- **`scripts/extract_one.js`** (1,591 bytes) -- Reads a single chat_history JSON file and prints all real messages (filtered of system messages) with sender labels and timestamps.

- **`scripts/extract_single_customer.js`** (4,274 bytes) -- Console-injectable script for manual extraction. Paste into Chrome DevTools Console on a customer detail page, auto-extracts all visible data fields and copies to clipboard.

#### Analysis Pipeline (S1-S2-S3)

- **`scripts/s1_classify.js`** (4,175 bytes) -- Stage 1: Metadata-based classification. Reads `tm_conversations_latest.json`, applies heuristic rules (unread, days since last contact, core market, system message detection), classifies all 466 conversations into P0-P4 tiers. Outputs to `data/s1_classification.json`.

- **`scripts/s1_msgcount.js`** (1,831 bytes) -- Counts messages per conversation from chat_history files.

- **`scripts/s2_deep.js`** (9,962 bytes) -- Stage 2: Deep analysis. Reads actual chat_history JSON files for a hardcoded list of 36 P0/P1 candidate names. For each: filters system messages, calculates message direction stats, detects 12 signal categories (PRICE, ORDER, PI, SHIPPING, PAYMENT, PRODUCT, SAMPLE, QTY, DECISION_CHAIN, URGENT, etc.), determines conversation stage (PI_SENT_WAITING_PAYMENT, NEGOTIATING, QUOTE_SENT, etc.), reassesses priority. Outputs to `data/s2_analysis.json`.

- **`scripts/s2_timeline.js`** (3,804 bytes) -- Extracts timeline of all messages for a conversation.

- **`scripts/s3_report.js`** (24,257 bytes) -- Stage 3: Word report generation v1. Contains hardcoded P0 (9 customers) and P1 (14 customers) analysis with reasons, blockers, next steps, and language-specific scripts (Spanish, French, Arabic, English). Uses `docx` npm package to generate formatted Word document with colored sections, tables, action items. Outputs to `reports/客户五梯队分析_2026-05-08.docx`.

- **`scripts/s3_report_v2.js`** (20,749 bytes) -- Stage 3 v2 with corrected P0 analysis (deeper dive into diego vargas, ali Amjid, jonathan aston, Aloh Voma, etc.) based on actual chat_history reads rather than metadata guesswork.

#### Rule Engine / Preprocessing

- **`scripts/preprocess_cdp.js`** (11,990 bytes) -- Stage 0: Data preprocessing. Detects system messages in `latestMessageContent`, replaces with first real message, marks emoji/single-word messages for lookback, counts messages, scans for product codes and address keywords, calculates silence days.

- **`scripts/classify_rules.js`** (10,728 bytes) -- Stage 1: Rule engine pre-classification. 13 deterministic rules covering: pure system messages -> P4, never-replied + 30d -> P4, 90d+ silence -> P3, unread + upgrade signals -> P0, etc. Outputs `data/classified_rules_*.json` with `needAI` flags.

- **`scripts/classify_ai.js`** (17,581 bytes) -- AI-assisted classification (complement to classify_rules.js).

- **`scripts/data-cleaner.mjs`** (6,044 bytes) -- Data cleaning module for the deep_scrape output.

#### Report Generation

- **`scripts/generate_report.js`** (14,175 bytes) -- Stage 5: Generic Word report generator using `docx` package. Supports colored headers (red for P0, orange for P1), zebra-striped tables, font hierarchy, page headers/footers with logo.

- **`scripts/generate-battle-report.mjs`** (28,763 bytes) -- Daily battle report generator (older version).

- Multiple older report generators: `generate-action-plan.mjs`, `generate-daily-report.mjs`, `generate-weekly-report.mjs`, `gen-batch-report.mjs`, `gen-peggy-report.mjs`, `gen-report-final.mjs`, `gen-status-report.mjs`, `final-report.mjs`.

#### TM Chat Exploration (Phase 1 series)

About 20+ scripts under `scripts/phase1_*.js` (dated 2026-05-05) exploring TM chat API access via various approaches:
- `phase1_tm_recon.js` through `phase1_tm_recon_v7.js` -- Progressive iterations trying to find TM chat API
- `phase1_call_http_api.js`, `phase1_http_api.js`, `phase1_http_fetch.js`, `phase1_http_messages.js` -- HTTP API approaches
- `phase1_explore_sdk.js`, `phase1_explore_services.js`, `phase1_explore_tms.js` -- SDK/service exploration
- `phase1_get_messages.js`, `phase1_get_messages_v2.js` -- Message retrieval attempts
- `phase1_save_full_data.js` -- Full data capture attempt
- `phase1_final_attempt.js` -- Final attempt at direct API call
- `phase1_click_contact.js`, `phase1_reconnect.js`, `phase1_reload.js` -- UI interaction approaches

#### WebSocket / LWP (Lightweight Web Protocol)

- `scripts/lwp_scrape_all.js` (12,343 bytes) -- Full LWP scraping via WebSocket hijacking
- `scripts/lwp_fetch.js` (3,250 bytes) -- LWP-based message fetching
- `scripts/hijack_ws.js` (6,952 bytes) -- WebSocket hijacking for message capture
- `scripts/ws_intercept.js` (2,899 bytes) -- WS frame interception
- `scripts/sniff_ws_frames.js` (4,475 bytes) -- Passive WS frame sniffing
- `scripts/explore_paas.js` (6,726 bytes) -- PAAS layer exploration
- `scripts/diagnose_ws.js`, `diagnose_ws2.js`, `diagnose_ws3.js` -- WS diagnostics

#### Deep Scraping (Older generations)

- `scripts/deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs` (15,835 bytes) -- DOM-based P0/P1 deep scraping with sidebar scrolling to load all virtual-scrolled contacts
- `scripts/deep-scrape-all.mjs`, `deep-scrape-all-sections.mjs` -- Full DOM-based scraping
- `scripts/deep-scrape-api.mjs` (12,743 bytes) -- API-based deep scraping
- `scripts/deep-scrape-dom.mjs` (8,977 bytes) -- DOM extraction approach
- `scripts/deep-scrape-v3.mjs` (10,859 bytes) -- v3 iteration
- `scripts/deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs` (14,704 bytes) -- P0-only focused variant

#### Utilities / Diagnostics

- `scripts/check_login.js` (2,073 bytes) -- Login state detection
- `scripts/debug_conversations.js` (2,842 bytes) -- Conversation data debugging
- `scripts/diagnose_api.js` (5,341 bytes) -- API endpoint diagnostics
- `scripts/network_sniff.js` through `network_sniff3.js` -- Network traffic sniffing
- `scripts/find_api_urls.js` (2,616 bytes) -- API URL discovery
- `scripts/test_*.js` (13 files) -- Various test scripts for message fetching, reconnection, flow testing
- `scripts/launch_chrome.js` (1,335 bytes) -- Chrome launcher helper
- `scripts/safe_extract.js` (10,625 bytes) -- Safe extraction with checks

---

### 3. KNOWLEDGE DIRECTORY (`knowledge/`)

Four core knowledge files loaded by Claude before analysis:

- **`knowledge/factory.md`** (2,607 bytes) -- Xiaoxingxing Garment Factory profile: 6000 sqm, 200+ employees, Yiyang city. content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Here is the full report.

---

## OKKI CRM Customer Analysis Project -- Complete Architecture Report

### Project Root
`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\`

**Purpose**: Customer analysis system for Xiaoxingxing Garment Factory (小星星服饰工厂), a B2B baby/children textile OEM/ODM factory in Yiyang, China. The project scrapes data from OKKI CRM (Alibaba's Xiaoman CRM), analyzes customer conversations, classifies them into a 5-tier priority system (P0-P4), and generates actionable daily follow-up reports.

---

### 1. KEY DOCUMENTATION FILES

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\CLAUDE.md`** (5855 bytes)
The master instruction file for Claude Code. Defines:
- **Account safety rules**: Sub-account (user) vs. main account (colleague) isolation. CDP-only approach to avoid triggering SSO token cascading failures.
- **Data scraping flow**: Close Chrome, run `node scripts/scrape_okki.js`, uses Profile 4 cookies for pre-authenticated session.
- **Thinking partner mode**: Claude is instructed to act as a thinking partner, not a tool -- challenges assumptions, identifies blind spots, provides evidence-based analysis, pushes back on user judgments when data contradicts them.
- **Analysis execution rules**: Read STATE.md first, then all knowledge/ files, batch constraint of <=5 customers per batch with <=500 total messages.
- **Data verification rules** (added 2026-05-08): Anti-hallucination checks -- verify statistics before reporting, never trust `latestMessage` fields for "has customer replied" judgments, spot-check 3 samples before reporting numbers.

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\STATE.md`** (3121 bytes)
The project state tracker (auto-loaded by SessionStart hook). Current state:
- **466 total conversations** (+13 from previous 453)
- **21 changed conversations**, **1788 new messages** in latest sync (2026-05-08)
- **14 customers replied** (118 messages), 159 messages sent by our side
- Latest analysis result: P0=9, P1=14, P2=26, P3=67, P4=355
- Key findings: diego vargas (19 customer msgs, only 1 reply -- critical oversight), Mona almuharraqy (846 messages, most active but unconverted), multiple PIs awaiting payment

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\DESIGN.md`** (9511 bytes)
Full architectural design document (2026-05-04). Defines the 6-stage pipeline:
- Stage 0: Data preprocessing (Node.js) -- system message filtering, emoji/single-word detection, silence day calculation
- Stage 1: Rule engine pre-classification (Node.js) -- deterministic rules for P3/P4, marks uncertain cases `needAI: true`
- Stage 2: AI classification with two-pass method -- metadata pass first, then full content pass
- Stage 3: P0+P1 deep analysis (customer background research, 5-step analysis, personalized scripts)
- Stage 4: Full lifecycle monitoring (status machine with 14 customer states)
- Stage 5: Word report generation via `docx` npm package

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\RUNBOOK.md`** (4493 bytes)
Operations manual with 4-step workflow: API batch scrape -> TM chat exploration -> batch AI analysis -> summary report. Documents verified APIs (`customerV3Read/companyList`, `customerRead/trailList`, `customerV2Read/detail`) and session planning.

**`d:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\PHASE1_执行指令.md`** (4493 bytes)
Phase 1 TM chat API reconnaissance instructions. Step-by-step for manually finding Alibaba MTOP API endpoints, payload formats, and response structures in Chrome DevTools.

---

### 2. SCRIPTS DIRECTORY (`scripts/`)

~110 script files total. Here are the currently active/key ones, organized by function:

#### Primary Data Scraping

- **`scripts/sync_okki.js`** (20,650 bytes) -- THE MAIN SCRIPT. One-click incremental sync. Automates: CDP port detection -> Chrome launch/connect -> login detection with wait -> navigate to onetalk iframe -> extract 466 conversation metadata from `window.__conversationListFullData__` -> diff against previous `tm_conversations_full.json` -> incrementally pull chat history via LWP (Lightweight Web Protocol) over WebSocket for changed conversations. Uses CDP `Runtime.evaluate` to send LWP requests through the captured WebSocket. Saves `tm_conversations_latest.json` and updates `chat_history/*.json`.

- **`scripts/scrape_okki.js`** (6,891 bytes) -- Playwright-based general scraper using Profile 4. Intercepts all OKKI API responses, captures customer lists, supports optional customer name search. Continuous mode with 30-second auto-save intervals.

- **`scripts/scrape_api_batch.js`** (6,672 bytes) -- Batch API scraper for customer detail + email trails. Connects via CDP, calls `customerRead/trailList` and `customerV2Read/detail` APIs via `page.evaluate()` XHR. Produces `data/customers/{company_id}.json` files (346 customer files). Supports `--since` and `--ids` flags.

- **`scripts/scrape_cdp.js`** (4,142 bytes) / **`scripts/scrape_cdp_full.js`** (3,191 bytes) -- CDP-based scrapers for customer list and detail data via Chrome DevTools Protocol.

- **`scripts/quick_sync.js`** (5,943 bytes) -- Lightweight v4 sync. Opens Chrome Profile 4, waits for manual login, navigates to onetalk, intercepts both HTTP responses and WebSocket frames, saves combined capture to `data/quicksync_*.json`.

- **`scripts/extract_cdp.js`** (8,294 bytes) -- CDP-based extraction via Chrome remote debugging. Reads DOM, executes JS, intercepts HTTP responses without triggering SSO.

- **`scripts/extract_playwright.js`** (7,884 bytes) -- Playwright-based extraction. Programmatically navigates customer pages, intercepts XHR/fetch, captures API responses.

- **`scripts/extract_one.js`** (1,591 bytes) -- Reads a single chat_history JSON file and prints all real messages (filtered of system messages) with sender labels and timestamps.

- **`scripts/extract_single_customer.js`** (4,274 bytes) -- Console-injectable script for manual extraction. Paste into Chrome DevTools Console on a customer detail page, auto-extracts all visible data fields and copies to clipboard.

#### Analysis Pipeline (S1-S2-S3)

- **`scripts/s1_classify.js`** (4,175 bytes) -- Stage 1: Metadata-based classification. Reads `tm_conversations_latest.json`, applies heuristic rules (unread, days since last contact, core market, system message detection), classifies all 466 conversations into P0-P4 tiers. Outputs to `data/s1_classification.json`.

- **`scripts/s1_msgcount.js`** (1,831 bytes) -- Counts messages per conversation from chat_history files.

- **`scripts/s2_deep.js`** (9,962 bytes) -- Stage 2: Deep analysis. Reads actual chat_history JSON files for a hardcoded list of 36 P0/P1 candidate names. For each: filters system messages, calculates message direction stats, detects 12 signal categories (PRICE, ORDER, PI, SHIPPING, PAYMENT, PRODUCT, SAMPLE, QTY, DECISION_CHAIN, URGENT, etc.), determines conversation stage (PI_SENT_WAITING_PAYMENT, NEGOTIATING, QUOTE_SENT, etc.), reassesses priority. Outputs to `data/s2_analysis.json`.

- **`scripts/s2_timeline.js`** (3,804 bytes) -- Extracts timeline of all messages for a conversation.

- **`scripts/s3_report.js`** (24,257 bytes) -- Stage 3: Word report generation v1. Contains hardcoded P0 (9 customers) and P1 (14 customers) analysis with reasons, blockers, next steps, and language-specific scripts (Spanish, French, Arabic, English). Uses `docx` npm package to generate formatted Word document with colored sections, tables, action items. Outputs to `reports/客户五梯队分析_2026-05-08.docx`.

- **`scripts/s3_report_v2.js`** (20,749 bytes) -- Stage 3 v2 with corrected P0 analysis (deeper dive into diego vargas, ali Amjid, jonathan aston, Aloh Voma, etc.) based on actual chat_history reads rather than metadata guesswork.

#### Rule Engine / Preprocessing

- **`scripts/preprocess_cdp.js`** (11,990 bytes) -- Stage 0: Data preprocessing. Detects system messages in `latestMessageContent`, replaces with first real message, marks emoji/single-word messages for lookback, counts messages, scans for product codes and address keywords, calculates silence days.

- **`scripts/classify_rules.js`** (10,728 bytes) -- Stage 1: Rule engine pre-classification. 13 deterministic rules covering: pure system messages -> P4, never-replied + 30d -> P4, 90d+ silence -> P3, unread + upgrade signals -> P0, etc. Outputs `data/classified_rules_*.json` with `needAI` flags.

- **`scripts/classify_ai.js`** (17,581 bytes) -- AI-assisted classification (complement to classify_rules.js).

- **`scripts/data-cleaner.mjs`** (6,044 bytes) -- Data cleaning module for the deep_scrape output.

#### Report Generation

- **`scripts/generate_report.js`** (14,175 bytes) -- Stage 5: Generic Word report generator using `docx` package. Supports colored headers (red for P0, orange for P1), zebra-striped tables, font hierarchy, page headers/footers with logo.

- **`scripts/generate-battle-report.mjs`** (28,763 bytes) -- Daily battle report generator (older version).

- Multiple older report generators: `generate-action-plan.mjs`, `generate-daily-report.mjs`, `generate-weekly-report.mjs`, `gen-batch-report.mjs`, `gen-peggy-report.mjs`, `gen-report-final.mjs`, `gen-status-report.mjs`, `final-report.mjs`.

#### TM Chat Exploration (Phase 1 series)

About 20+ scripts under `scripts/phase1_*.js` (dated 2026-05-05) exploring TM chat API access via various approaches:
- `phase1_tm_recon.js` through `phase1_tm_recon_v7.js` -- Progressive iterations trying to find TM chat API
- `phase1_call_http_api.js`, `phase1_http_api.js`, `phase1_http_fetch.js`, `phase1_http_messages.js` -- HTTP API approaches
- `phase1_explore_sdk.js`, `phase1_explore_services.js`, `phase1_explore_tms.js` -- SDK/service exploration
- `phase1_get_messages.js`, `phase1_get_messages_v2.js` -- Message retrieval attempts
- `phase1_save_full_data.js` -- Full data capture attempt
- `phase1_final_attempt.js` -- Final attempt at direct API call
- `phase1_click_contact.js`, `phase1_reconnect.js`, `phase1_reload.js` -- UI interaction approaches

#### WebSocket / LWP (Lightweight Web Protocol)

- `scripts/lwp_scrape_all.js` (12,343 bytes) -- Full LWP scraping via WebSocket hijacking
- `scripts/lwp_fetch.js` (3,250 bytes) -- LWP-based message fetching
- `scripts/hijack_ws.js` (6,952 bytes) -- WebSocket hijacking for message capture
- `scripts/ws_intercept.js` (2,899 bytes) -- WS frame interception
- `scripts/sniff_ws_frames.js` (4,475 bytes) -- Passive WS frame sniffing
- `scripts/explore_paas.js` (6,726 bytes) -- PAAS layer exploration
- `scripts/diagnose_ws.js`, `diagnose_ws2.js`, `diagnose_ws3.js` -- WS diagnostics

#### Deep Scraping (Older generations)

- `scripts/deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs` (15,835 bytes) -- DOM-based P0/P1 deep scraping with sidebar scrolling to load all virtual-scrolled contacts
- `scripts/deep-scrape-all.mjs`, `deep-scrape-all-sections.mjs` -- Full DOM-based scraping
- `scripts/deep-scrape-api.mjs` (12,743 bytes) -- API-based deep scraping
- `scripts/deep-scrape-dom.mjs` (8,977 bytes) -- DOM extraction approach
- `scripts/deep-scrape-v3.mjs` (10,859 bytes) -- v3 iteration
- `scripts/deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs` (14,704 bytes) -- P0-only focused variant

#### Utilities / Diagnostics

- `scripts/check_login.js` (2,073 bytes) -- Login state detection
- `scripts/debug_conversations.js` (2,842 bytes) -- Conversation data debugging
- `scripts/diagnose_api.js` (5,341 bytes) -- API endpoint diagnostics
- `scripts/network_sniff.js` through `network_sniff3.js` -- Network traffic sniffing
- `scripts/find_api_urls.js` (2,616 bytes) -- API URL discovery
- `scripts/test_*.js` (13 files) -- Various test scripts for message fetching, reconnection, flow testing
- `scripts/launch_chrome.js` (1,335 bytes) -- Chrome launcher helper
- `scripts/safe_extract.js` (10,625 bytes) -- Safe extraction with checks

---

| `完整档案_2026-04-28T11-50-19.docx` | ~22 KB | 2026-04-28 complete archive |
| `看板报告_2026-04-28T11-18-57.docx` | ~13 KB | 2026-04-28 dashboard report |
| `数据抓取验证报告_2026-05-05.md` | ~7 KB | Data scraping verification report |
| `外贸B2B跟进SOP参考.md` | ~5 KB | B2B follow-up SOP reference |
| `CHECKPOINT.md` | ~3 KB | Session checkpoint from 2026-04-28 |

---

### 6. CLAUDE HOOKS (`.claude/`)

- **`hooks/load-state.js`** (SessionStart hook): Reads STATE.md and prints first 2000 characters into context on session start.
- **`hooks/update-state.js`** (Stop hook): Auto-updates the "最后更新" (last updated) timestamp in STATE.md on session end.
- **`settings.json`**: Registers the two hooks above.

---

### 7. ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY: HOW DATA FLOWS

```
Chrome Profile 4 (pre-authenticated)
    |
    v
[Scrape Layer] scripts/sync_okki.js (main) or scrape_okki.js / scrape_api_batch.js
    |-- HTTP response interception (OKKI APIs)
    |-- WebSocket hijacking (LWP protocol for TM chat messages)
    |-- CDP Runtime.evaluate for DOM/JS data extraction
    |
    v
[Raw Data] data/
    |-- tm_conversations_latest.json     (466 conversation metadata)
    |-- data/chat_history/*.json         (467 chat history files)
    |-- data/customers/*.json            (346 customer detail files)
    |
    v
[Classification Pipeline]
    Stage 1 (S1): s1_classify.js -- metadata heuristic → P0-P4 candidates
    Stage 2 (S2): s2_deep.js     -- read chat_history, detect signals, reassess
    Stage 3 (S3): s3_report.js   -- hardcoded P0/P1 analysis → Word .docx
    |
    v
[Output] reports/客户五梯队分析_2026-05-08.docx
```

**Key technical details:**
- **Self UID**: `2500000853693@icbu`
- **Message content path**: `message.content.text.content`
- **Sender path**: `message.sender.uid`
- **Timestamp path**: `message.createAt` (milliseconds)
- **System message filter**: Both `extension.basicMessageInfo` JSON flag (`systemMessage: true` / `autoReply: true`) AND 14 text patterns
- **Profile used**: Chrome Profile 4 (`C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Google\Chrome\User Data\Profile 4`)
- **OKKI CRM URL**: `https://crm.xiaoman.cn/`
- **Onetalk (TM chat) URL**: `https://onetalk.alibaba.com/message/weblitePWA.htm`
- **LWP endpoint**: `/r/MessageManager/listUserMessages` with params `[cid, false, cursor, count, false]`
- **Known APIs**: `customerV3Read/companyList`, `customerRead/trailList`, `customerV2Read/detail`
- **Current data volumes**: 466 conversations, ~467 chat history files, 346 customer detail files

lastPrompt: 你想想该方案有没有更好的办法

content.thinking:
Excellent. Now I have the complete picture. The user has a very sophisticated OKKI scraping infrastructure already built:

1. `sync_okki.js` — main incremental sync script that can pull conversation data automatically
2. 467 chat history JSON files — all customer conversations
3. 346 customer detail JSON files — customer metadata
4. Classification pipeline (S1 → S2 → S3) that categorizes customers into P0-P4
5. Report generation in .docx format

The user is asking me to think of a better approach than the original "OB mirrors OKKI data" scheme. Given what's already built, let me think about what would genuinely be better:

**Key insight from the exploration**: The data infrastructure is already good. JSON files are auto-generated, structured, and CC can read them. The classification pipeline already identifies who needs attention (P0/P1).

The problem with the original OB scheme was: **dual-entry** — maintaining OB notes that mirror OKKI data.

But now I see that the data is already in JSON. So the "better way" is:

**Don't mirror data. Use JSON as the data layer, and use OB only for the "strategy/decision" layer that the JSON can't capture.**

But even beyond that, let me think about what would make the workflow genuinely better:

1. **Current workflow**: User runs sync → CC analyzes → generates static report. But between syncs, things change. Customer replies come in. The report goes stale.

2. **The real problem**: How to keep CC informed between syncs without the user doing heavy manual data entry.

**Better approaches I can think of:**

### Approach A: "Paste-and-Go" — Zero maintenance, on-demand context
When a customer messages in OKKI:
1. User copies the message text
2. Pastes to CC: "Ahmed回了：[paste]"  
3. CC reads the customer's JSON chat history + customer profile (already scraped)
4. CC drafts reply based on full context
5. User sends reply in OKKI
6. No OB maintenance at all — JSON data IS the context

The only OB usage: when CC discovers something worth remembering (a pattern, a decision, a customer preference), CC writes ONE line to an OB note. Otherwise, nothing.

This is fundamentally different from the original scheme because:
- No dual-entry for data (JSON is auto-scraped)
- OB is write-only by CC, read by both
- User never manually updates OB for customers
- The heavy data (chat history) stays in JSON, which CC reads on demand per-customer

### Approach B: "Daily Dashboard" — Auto-generated situational awareness
Every day, CC:
1. Reads `s1_classification.json` (already generated by sync)
2. Reads the 9 P0 customer JSON files (new messages only)
3. Generates a 1-page dashboard: who messaged, who went silent too long, what needs action today
4. Writes to OB as `今日跟进.md`

User opens OB in the morning, sees the dashboard, knows exactly what to do.
The dashboard is auto-generated from JSON data, not manually maintained.

### Approach C: "Signal Detection" — Automated anomaly alerts
CC compares today's data snapshot vs. yesterday's:
- "Ahmed was about to pay (PI sent 3 days ago), still hasn't paid → flag"
- "Fatima went from active to 7 days silent → flag"
- "New customer Jose has high-value signals (multiple product inquiries) → flag for fast response"

These are written to a `预警.md` in OB.

### Approach D: The truly radical one — CC as the OKKI interface
Instead of the user going to OKKI → copying → pasting to CC:
1. User runs `sync_okki.js` (or it runs on a schedule)
2. CC reads the fresh data directly
3. User asks CC: "今天该做什么？"
4. CC: "3个人需要跟进：Ahmed（样品已签收3天该推进了）、Fatima（PI发出7天未付款）、Jose（新客户高意向，24h内必须回复）"
5. User: "帮我看Ahmed" → CC reads Ahmed's JSON → full context
6. User: "帮我起草给Ahmed的回复" → CC drafts
7. User copies reply, sends in OKKI
8. End of day: "记录：已回复Ahmed推进试单" → CC writes one line to a log

This means:
- OB stores: daily action logs, strategy decisions, templates
- JSON stores: all raw customer data (auto-sync)
- CC bridges: reads JSON for facts, reads OB for strategy, generates actions

### My recommendation

The "better way" is actually a combination:

**Data layer**: JSON files (already built, auto-synced, zero maintenance)
- CC reads on demand per customer
- Never loads all 467 files at once

**Strategy layer**: OB with 2-3 files max
- `客户策略.md` — per-customer strategy notes (only for P0/P1, ~20 customers, one section each)
- `跟进日志.md` — daily action log (CC writes one line per action)
- `信号库.md` — learned patterns (e.g., "中东客户周五下午回复率高")

**CC workflow**: 
1. Read fresh JSON data
2. Cross-reference with OB strategy
3. Generate dashboard/actions
4. Update OB log

This eliminates dual-entry entirely. The JSON data flows automatically. OB only stores what the JSON can't: decisions, strategies, lessons learned.

Let me present this clearly to the user, focusing on why this is better than the original "OB mirrors OKKI" approach.

Actually, the user's question was very specific: "你想想该方案有没有更好的办法" — referring to the OKKI customer followup + forgetting-alert scheme. Let me focus my answer on that specific scheme and present the better approach.

Let me write a concise but insightful response.

content.text:
你现在的基础设施比我之前假设的**成熟得多**。看完整个项目后，我之前提的"OB 里给每个客户建笔记"方案确实不够好——你已经有 467 个 JSON 聊天记录、346 个客户档案、一套完整的分类管线。再去 OB 里手工建一套镜像数据，是**开倒车**。

更好的办法是换一个架构思路：

---

## 不是"OB 镜像 OKKI 数据"，而是"JSON 管事实，OB 管决策"

你的 `sync_okki.js` 已经能增量同步 466 个会话的聊天记录到 JSON。这才是数据层。OB 不应该去复制它，而应该存**JSON 表达不了的东西**。

具体来说三层分工：

| 层 | 存储 | 内容 | 谁维护 |
|---|------|------|--------|
| **数据层** | `data/chat_history/*.json` | 聊天记录、客户档案 | `sync_okki.js` 自动跑 |
| **决策层** | OB `客户策略.md` | P0/P1 客户的策略判断、异常信号、历史决策 | CC 自动写，你偶尔改 |
| **日志层** | OB `跟进日志.md` | 每天做了什么、结果如何 | CC 一句话追加 |

**关键变化**：你不需要手工维护任何东西。`sync_okki.js` 拉数据，CC 读完 JSON 后自动对比、自动写 OB。

---

## 实际工作流变成这样

**每天早上**：
- 你：`node scripts/sync_okki.js`（30 秒）
- 你：对 CC 说"更新今日跟进"
- CC：读 `s1_classification.json` + P0/P1 的 chat_history JSON → 对比 OB 里的策略记录 → 自动生成今日仪表盘，标注异常（"Ahmed 样品签收 3 天没推进""Fatima PI 发出 7 天未付"）
- CC 把仪表盘写到 `OB/今日跟进.md`

**客户回复时**：
- 你：在 OKKI 看到新消息，复制粘贴给 CC"Ahmed回了：[paste]"
- CC：读 `Ahmed.json`（已有）+ OB 策略 → 30 秒出回复草稿
- 你：复制到 OKKI 发送
- 你：对 CC 说"记录" → CC 在 OB 日志追加一行

**周末回顾时**：
- 你：对 CC 说"本周跟进总结"
- CC：读 OB 日志 + JSON 数据 → 自动出"本周回了多少人、几个推进了、几个沉默了、下周重点"

---

## 和原方案相比好在哪

1. **零双录**：聊天数据 sync 脚本自动拉，不需要从 OKKI 复制到 OB
2. **OB 极轻**：只需维护 2-3 个文件（策略 + 日志 + 仪表盘），不是 432 篇笔记
3. **CC 读得少**：日常只读 JSON 索引 + 当日涉及的那几个客户 JSON，上下文几乎不占
4. **策略会积累**：CC 每次发现新规律（"这个客户对价格极度敏感""中东客户周五下午回复率高"）自动追加到策略文件，越用越聪明

---

要我把这个方案落到 OKKI 项目里吗？改动不大：加一个 `knowledge/customer-strategy.md` 模板 + 一个 CC 启动指令，不改现有脚本。

根据搜索结果，我来为您梳理这几个关键词的关联信息：

---

## 搜索结果总结

### 1. 「大力AI」与抖音
搜索结果中**未直接找到**名为"大力AI"的抖音账号与 Claude Code 的关联内容。搜索中出现的"大力"主要指向字节跳动旗下的**大力教育**（教育硬件品牌），这与 AI 编程工具 Claude Code 并无直接关系。如果您指的是某位抖音上的 AI 博主，可能需要更具体的账号名称。

### 2. Claude Code 的「大脑」概念
来自抖音博主"**金黄说AI**"的一期视频中，详细讲解了 Claude Code 的核心架构：

> **Agent = 大脑 + 工具**
> - **大脑**：大语言模型提供智力基础，负责思考和判断
> - **工具**：让大脑能够实际操作的手脚（读写文件、执行代码、操控浏览器等）
> - Claude Code 不只是编程工具，它能完成电脑 **90%-95%** 的操作任务
> - 支持自然语言交互，未来可能替代鼠标键盘

### 3. Oh-My-Claude / Oh-My-Claudecode
这是一个由韩国开发者 **Yeachan-Heo** 创建的 Claude Code 多智能体编排插件（GitHub 11,000+ Stars），相当于给 Claude Code 装上了真正的「大脑」：

| 功能 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **32 个专业 Agent** | 架构、开发、测试、数据分析等 |
| **5 种执行模式** | Autopilot（自主）、Ultrapilot（3-5倍并行）、Ecomode（省钱）、Swarm（协同）、Pipeline（顺序） |
| **智能模型路由** | 简单任务用 Haiku，复杂用 Opus，节省 30-50% token |
| **魔法关键词** | `autopilot`、`ralph`、`ulw`、`eco` 等快捷指令 |
| **HUD 状态栏** | 实时显示执行进度和 Token 使用 |

**安装方式：**
```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode
/plugin install oh-my-claudecode
```

**使用示例：**
```

根据搜索结果，我来为您梳理这几个关键词的关联信息：

---

## 搜索结果总结

### 1. 「大力AI」与抖音
搜索结果中**未直接找到**名为"大力AI"的抖音账号与 Claude Code 的关联内容。搜索中出现的"大力"主要指向字节跳动旗下的**大力教育**（教育硬件品牌），这与 AI 编程工具 Claude Code 并无直接关系。如果您指的是某位抖音上的 AI 博主，可能需要更具体的账号名称。

### 2. Claude Code 的「大脑」概念
来自抖音博主"**金黄说AI**"的一期视频中，详细讲解了 Claude Code 的核心架构：

> **Agent = 大脑 + 工具**
> - **大脑**：大语言模型提供智力基础，负责思考和判断
> - **工具**：让大脑能够实际操作的手脚（读写文件、执行代码、操控浏览器等）
> - Claude Code 不只是编程工具，它能完成电脑 **90%-95%** 的操作任务
> - 支持自然语言交互，未来可能替代鼠标键盘

### 3. Oh-My-Claude / Oh-My-Claudecode
这是一个由韩国开发者 **Yeachan-Heo** 创建的 Claude Code 多智能体编排插件（GitHub 11,000+ Stars），相当于给 Claude Code 装上了真正的「大脑」：

| 功能 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **32 个专业 Agent** | 架构、开发、测试、数据分析等 |
| **5 种执行模式** | Autopilot（自主）、Ultrapilot（3-5倍并行）、Ecomode（省钱）、Swarm（协同）、Pipeline（顺序） |
| **智能模型路由** | 简单任务用 Haiku，复杂用 Opus，节省 30-50% token |
| **魔法关键词** | `autopilot`、`ralph`、`ulw`、`eco` 等快捷指令 |
| **HUD 状态栏** | 实时显示执行进度和 Token 使用 |

**安装方式：**
```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode
/plugin install oh-my-claudecode
```

**使用示例：**
```
autopilot: build a REST API for managing tasks
ulw: fix all errors in the codebase
eco: migrate database schema
```

## oh-my-claudecode (OMC) — by Yeachan-Heo

**OMC** is a **teams-first multi-agent orchestration layer for Claude Code**, featuring **32 specialized agents** with smart model routing, persistent execution loops, and real-time HUD visibility. It's designed to have zero learning curve.

---

### Key Features (2025)

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| **32 Specialized Agents** | 32 agents with smart model routing (Haiku → Opus) for different task types |
| **Team Mode** | Staged pipeline: `team-plan → team-prd → team-exec → team-verify → team-fix` (looping until verified) |
| **Autopilot** | Autonomous single lead agent for end-to-end feature work with minimal ceremony |
| **Ultrapilot** | Legacy facade that routes to Team mode |
| **Ultrawork** | Maximum parallelism for burst parallel fixes/refactors |
| **Ralph** | Persistent mode with verify/fix loops — guaranteed completion |
| **Pipeline** | Sequential staged processing for multi-step transformations |
| **Swarm** | Legacy facade (routes to Team) |
| **Cost Optimization** | Smart model routing saves 30–50% on tokens |
| **Notifications** | Telegram/Discord callbacks when execution completes |
| **Rate Limit Recovery** | Auto-resume daemon (`omc wait`) for when rate limits reset |

---

### Orchestration Modes

| Mode | Use Case |
|---|---|
| **Team** (recommended) | Coordinated agents on shared task list |
| **Autopilot** | End-to-end feature work, minimal ceremony |
| **Ultrawork** | Maximum parallelism for fixes/refactors |
| **Ralph** | Tasks that must complete fully (includes ultrawork) |
| **Pipeline** | Multi-step transformations |
| **Swarm/Ultrapilot** | Legacy — route to Team mode |

---

### Magic Keywords

- `autopilot: <task>` — Full autonomous execution
- `ralph: <task>` — Persistent execution mode
- `ulw <task>` — Maximum parallelism
- `team` — Canonical Team orchestration
- `ultrapilot: <task>` — Legacy, routes to Team

---

### Installation

```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode
/plugin install oh-my-claudecode
/omc:omc-setup
```

Or via npm: `npm install -g oh-my-claude-sisyphus`

The project is available on GitHub at **github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode** and is listed on the Tessl skills registry.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
